Antonio Conte claims Sean Dyche has it easy at Burnley compared to Chelsea job

Conte explained: "I think he (Dyche) is doing an important job. He is doing a great job this season. But if you start the season trying to fight to avoid the relegation zone it is more simple.

"It is more simple also if you have a small budget because you can count on a team that in the past you qualify to play again in the league.

"It is more difficult when you have to prepare the transfer market to win something because only one team wins. The others must see the other team win."

Conte believes ending the campaign having merely survived in the top flight is far less of an achievement.

He added: "It is more simple because you have to avoid the last three places at the bottom and then you can stay between 10 teams. You can play only for this target.

Leon Bailey's Leverkusen Future in Doubt After Agent and Adoptive Father's Furious Social Media Rant

Bayer Leverkusen winger Leon Bailey could leave the club this summer, even though the player's contract runs until 2022, after an extraordinary rant from his father and agent on Instagram.


According to the Mirror, the Bundesliga club would be unlikely to reject an offer of more than £35m for the 20-year-old, who has taken German football by storm since moving to the BayArena from Belgian side Genk last year.

Mirror: https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/football/news/leon-baileys-future-bayer-leverkusen-12391049

Bailey has netted 12 goals this season, helping to propel Leverkusen to third place in the Bundesliga.

However, the club's relationship with Bailey's agent Craig Butler - who is also his adoptive father - is bound to be extremely strained, after Butler publicly criticised the way Bailey was used in a 6-2 drubbing by Bayern Munich in the German Cup semi-final.


In a strongly-worded Instagram post, Butler wrote: "They put him on the bench. They put him on at left-back. They don't pass to him. The opponent double teams him. They kick him down. "What does he do? Gets up and put the free kick in the back of the damn net."

​In addition, Bailey has admitted that he aspires to move to a bigger club than Leverkusen, even though he is content at the BayArena for now. Speaking in February, he said: "Leverkusen is perfect for me. I want to develop and then I can take the next step. When I move to a big club, the people there will want to know me."


Although he is Jamaican-born and has represented the Reggae Boyz at Under-23 level, Bailey is yet to pledge his allegiance to any nation at senior level.

Among other countries, he is reportedly eligible for England, which has given rise to speculation that he may yet represent the Three Lions - though ​his agent has also reportedly claimed he could represent Germany.

Leon Bailey's future at Bayer Leverkusen in further question after adoptive father's social media blast

Leon Bailey's future at Bayer Leverkusen has been brought into question after his adoptive father and agent took aim at the club on social media.

Bailey, 20, has emerged as one of the hottest prospects in the Bundesliga since moving to Germany from Genk 15 months ago and has a growing list of Premier League admirers.

A number of clubs, including Manchester United and Chelsea, have sent scouts to watch the Jamaican starlet at the BayArena, while the FA have looked into whether Bailey is eligible to represent the Three Lions.

Bailey has scored 12 times for Leverkusen this term, with Heiko Herrlich’s side currently third in the German top tier, on course for a return to the Champions League.

But he found himself on the substitutes bench for Bayer’s German Cup semi-final clash with Bayern Munich in midweek, rested ahead of a crucial top four battle with Borussia Dortmund on Saturday.

The club's general manager Rudi Voller explained the decision, stating "he has recently looked a bit tired."

However, with Bayer trailing 2-1 at the break, coach Heiko Herrlich brought Bailey off the bench at half-time as he looked to get back into the game.

Bailey replaced left-back Panagiotis Retsos and duly played on the left side of defence as the German champions ran riot with an early second half blitz, scoring three times in 11 minutes to take the game away from the hosts.

However Bailey’s father, Craig Butler, took to Instagram to praise his son and fire on the club management, mocking the decision and tactics of Herrlich.

Butler also blasted teammates for not passing to his son.

Butler wrote on the social media platform: "They put him on the bench. They put him on at left-back. They don't pass to him. The opponent double teams him. They kick him down. "What does he do? Gets up and put the free kick in the back of the damn net."

Herrlich, in his first full season as Bayer boss, had little to say about Butler's thoughts: "I accept his opinion, that's it."

Bailey's deal at the BayArena runs until the summer of 2022, but Bayer will likely be unable to turn down offers in excess of £35million should they arrive this summer.

However, Bailey has admitted that he is happy to continue his development with Bayer right now, despite interest from elsewhere.
